findings.evidence: `rg` hits: `AbelJacobiSum.lean:121` proves `S.weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass w h hx₀ D = D.sum fun x n => n • S.weightedAbelJacobiClass w h hx₀ x`; `AbelJacobiSum.lean:282` proves the unweighted analogue; `FiniteSum.lean:210`, `:457`, and `:657` already expand `ofFinsupp`, `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity`, and `ofFinset` as the same finite sums over point divisors.
findings.fix: Replace these parallel named lemmas with uses of the existing `*_eq_sum` API plus the finite-constructor coefficient/support or sum lemmas, or add only a small generic bridge if that rewrite is repeatedly awkward.
findings.issue: All six new declarations duplicate special cases of the existing general formulas `weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass_eq_sum` and `unweightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass_eq_sum` for `D = ofFinsupp m`, `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity s m`, and `ofFinset s`.

summary: The new finite Abel-Jacobi lemmas are thin specializations of the existing divisor-level sum API rather than new reusable mathematics.
